menu.redbyte.robo_terminal.title: Edit Code
interpreter.redbyte.diagnostics.invalid_: Invalid binary operator '%1$s' for types '%2$s' and '%3$s'.
interpreter.redbyte.diagnostics.function: Could not find function '%1$s' with given arguments.
interpreter.redbyte.diagnostics.primitiv: A primitive value is not allowed here.
runtime.redbyte.error.unsupported_expres: This expression is not supported yet.
runtime.redbyte.error.unsupported_binary: This binary operation is not yet.
runtime.redbyte.error.unsupported_unary_: This unary expresion is not yet.
runtime.redbyte.error.variable_no_value: Variable '%1$s' does not have a value.
runtime.redbyte.error.expected_range: Expected value to be in range of '%1$s' and '%2$s'.
docs.redbyte.description.functions.print: Prints the argument into the console.
docs.redbyte.description.functions.is_no: Returns true if given argument is a non-existing value. Returns false otherwise.
docs.redbyte.description.functions.walk: Walks the given amount of blocks in look direction.
docs.redbyte.description.functions.rotat: Rotates around the y-axis with the given angle.
docs.redbyte.description.functions.get_p: Returns the player with given name if existing. Returns nothing otherwise.
docs.redbyte.description.functions.get_n: Returns the nearest player in given distance. Returns nothing if no player is in given distance.
Returns the nearest monster in given distance. Returns nothing if no monster is in given distance.
docs.redbyte.description.functions.get_b: Returns the block at given position.
docs.redbyte.description.functions.try_p: Tries to place the block at given slot position.
docs.redbyte.description.functions.vecto: Returns the distance between two vectors.
Looks at the direction of given vector.
docs.redbyte.description.functions.block: Looks at the direction of given block.
docs.redbyte.description.functions.creat: Follows given creature until stopped via 'stop_follow' method.
Attacks given creature if it is reachable and attackable.
Returns true if given creature is reachable and attackable. Returns false otherwise.
Looks at the direction of given creature.
docs.redbyte.description.properties.vect: Returns the x value of given vector.
Returns the y value of given vector.
Returns the z value of given vector.
docs.redbyte.description.properties.bloc: Returns the position of given block.
docs.redbyte.description.properties.enti: Returns the position of given entity.
docs.redbyte.description.properties.crea: Returns the current health of given creature.
docs.redbyte.title.docs1: These are the documentations of the Redbyte language.
docs.redbyte.title.docs2: At the top right corner you can select what topic you want to do research on.
docs.redbyte.title.name: Variable
docs.redbyte.explanation.block: The block statement puts multiple statements into one together.
docs.redbyte.explanation.expression: The expression statement is just a formality so expressions can be used as statements too.
docs.redbyte.explanation.if: The if statement checks if the given condition is true and executes the given statement if so. If an (optional) else clause was given then that part will be executed if given condition is false.
docs.redbyte.explanation.loop: The loop statement executes the statement exactly x times where x is the given count.
docs.redbyte.explanation.once: The once statement waits until given condition is true. If it is true, the given statement gets executed and the once statement is finished.
docs.redbyte.explanation.while: The while statement executes the given statement if the given condition is true. If the condition is once false, the executions of the while loop breaks.
docs.redbyte.explanation.always: The always statement is an infinite loop that always executes the given statement if the given condition is true.
syntax.redbyte.general.name: <name>
